abstract: This paper introduces an innovative collaborative game designed to crowdsource natural language referring expressions. By employing a two-player framework, we are able to simultaneously collect and verify expressions in a single seamless process. To date, this approach has yielded a large-scale dataset comprising 130,525 expressions for 96,654 distinct objects across 19,894 photographs of complex natural scenes. This dataset significantly surpasses previous Referring Expression Generation (REG) corpora in both scale and linguistic diversity, facilitating a more robust study of how humans describe objects in real-world contexts. We provide a comprehensive analysis of the collected data and, leveraging these insights, propose a novel optimization-based model for generating referring expressions. Finally, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through experimental evaluations across three diverse test sets.
